children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall implement early outreach activities in order to provide prospective students and their families with information about financial aid and estimates of financial aid. Such early outreach activities shall include the activities described in subsections (b), (c), and (d).

children.heading: Pell Grant early awareness estimates
children.label: b Pell Grant early awareness estimates
A
children.text: a single-parent household;

children.label: B
children.text: a household with two parents; or

children.label: C
children.text: a household with no children or dependents.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The method of estimating eligibility described in paragraph (1) shall be consumer tested with prospective first-generation students and families as well as low-income individuals and families.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: B Updates
children.text: For award year 2024–2025 and each fourth succeeding award year thereafter, the design of the method of estimating eligibility shall be updated based on additional consumer testing with the populations described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: made publicly and prominently available on the Department’s website; and

children.label: i
children.text: institutions of higher education participating in programs under this subchapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: all middle and secondary schools eligible for funds under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 6311

children.label: iii
children.text: local educational agencies and middle schools and high schools that serve students not less than 25 percent of whom meet a measure of poverty as described in section 1113(a)(5) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 []; and20 U.S.C. 6313(a)(5)

children.label: iv
children.text: agencies responsible for administering means-tested Federal benefit programs, as defined in .section 1087ss(b)(4)(H) of this title

children.heading: Electronic estimator on FAFSA
children.label: 4 Electronic estimator on FAFSA
children.text: In accordance with subsection (d)(5) of , the Secretary shall maintain an electronic method for applicants to enter income and family size, and level of education sought information to calculate a non-binding estimate (which may include a range, ceiling, or minimum) of the applicant’s Federal financial aid available under this subchapter and shall place such calculator on a prominent location on the FAFSA website and in a manner that encourages students to fill out the FAFSA.section 1090 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: all middle schools and secondary schools eligible for funds under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 6311

children.label: ii
children.text: local educational agencies and middle schools and high schools that serve students not less than 25 percent of whom meet a measure of poverty as described in section 1113(a)(5) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act [];20 U.S.C. 6313(a)(5)

children.label: iii
children.text: households receiving assistance under the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: iv
children.text: agencies responsible for administering means-tested Federal benefit programs, as defined in .section 1087ss(b)(4)(H) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the availability of the College Scorecard or any similar successor website;

children.label: II
children.text: the electronic estimates of financial aid available under subsection (b);

children.label: III
children.text: Federal financial aid available to students, including eligibility criteria for the Federal financial aid and an explanation of the Federal financial aid programs (including applicable Federal educational tax credits); and

children.label: IV
children.text: resources that can inform students of financial aid that may be available from state-based financial aid, state-based college savings programs, and scholarships and other non-governmental sources;

children.label: ii
children.text: describe how the dissemination of information will be conducted by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reporting and updates
children.label: C Reporting and updates
children.text: The Secretary shall post the information about the plans under subparagraph (A) and associated goals publicly on the Department’s website. On an annual basis, the Secretary shall report qualitative and quantitative outcomes regarding the implementation of the plans under subparagraph (A). The Secretary shall review and update such plans not less often than every 4 award years with the goal of progressively increasing the impact of the activities under this paragraph.

children.heading: Partnership
children.label: D Partnership
children.text: The Secretary may partner with States, State systems of higher education, institutions of higher education, or college access organizations to carry out this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Secretary of Labor.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture.

children.label: v
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs.

children.label: viii
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: i
children.text: identify opportunities in which low-income individuals and families could be informed of the availability of Federal financial aid under this subchapter through access to other Federal programs that serve low-income individuals and families;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify methods to effectively inform low-income individuals and families of the availability of Federal financial aid for postsecondary education under this subchapter and assist such individuals in completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid;

children.label: iii
children.text: develop early awareness and FAFSA completion activities that align with the opportunities and methods identified under clauses (i) and (ii);

children.label: iv
children.text: establish goals regarding the effects of the activities to be implemented under clause (iii); and

children.label: v
children.text: provide information on how students and families can maintain access to Federal programs that serve low-income individuals and families operated by the agencies identified under subsection (A) while attending an institution of higher education.

children.heading: Plan with Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: C Plan with Secretary of the Treasury
children.text: The interagency coordination plan under subparagraph (A)(i) between the Secretary and the Secretary of the Treasury shall further include specific methods to increase the application for Federal financial aid under this subchapter from individuals who file Federal tax returns, including collaboration with tax preparation entities or other third parties, as appropriate.

children.heading: Reporting and updates
children.label: D Reporting and updates
children.text: The Secretary shall post the information about the interagency coordination plans under this paragraph and associated goals publicly on the Department’s website. The plans shall have the goal of progressively increasing the impact of the activities under this paragraph by increasing the number of low-income applicants for, and recipients of, Federal financial aid. The plans shall be updated not less than once every 4 years.

children.heading: Nationwide participation in early awareness plans
children.label: 3 Nationwide participation in early awareness plans
i
children.text: notify prospective and existing students who are low-income individuals and families about their eligibility for Federal aid under this subchapter, as well as State-based financial aid, if applicable, on an annual basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: increase the number of prospective and current students who are low-income individuals and families filing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid; and

children.label: iii
children.text: increase the number of prospective and current students who are low-income individuals and families enrolling in postsecondary education.

children.heading: Reporting and updates
children.label: B Reporting and updates
children.text: Each entity that makes a voluntary public commitment to carry out an early awareness plan may submit quantitative and qualitative data based on the entity’s progress toward the goals of the plan annually prior to a date selected by the Secretary.

children.heading: Early awareness champions
children.label: C Early awareness champions
children.text: Based on data submitted by entities, the Secretary shall select and designate entities submitting public commitments, plans, and goals, as Early Awareness Champions on an annual basis. Those entities designated as Early Awareness Champions shall provide one or more case studies regarding the activities the entity undertook under this paragraph which shall be made public by the Secretary on the Department of Education website to promote best practices.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement a public awareness campaign designed using current and relevant independent research regarding strategies and media platforms found to be most effective in communicating with low-income populations in order to increase national awareness regarding the availability of Federal Pell Grants and financial aid under this subchapter and, at the option of the Secretary, potential availability of state need-based financial aid.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 2 Coordination
children.text: The public awareness campaign described in paragraph (1) shall leverage the activities in subsections (b) and (c) to highlight eligibility among low-income populations. In developing and implementing the campaign, the Secretary may work in coordination with States, institutions of higher education, early intervention and outreach programs under this subchapter, other Federal agencies, agencies responsible for administering means-tested Federal benefit programs (as defined in ), organizations involved in college access and student financial aid, secondary schools, local educational agencies, public libraries, community centers, businesses, employers, workforce investment boards, and organizations that provide services to individuals who are or were homeless, in foster care, or are disconnected youth.section 1087ss(b)(4)(H) of this title

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 3 Reporting
children.text: The Secretary shall report on the success of the public awareness campaign described in paragraph (1) annually regarding the extent to which the public and target populations were reached using data commonly used to evaluate advertising and outreach campaigns and data regarding whether the campaign produced any increase in applicants for Federal aid under this subchapter publicly on the Department of Education website.
